note: THE HONEST FRAME instruments (Directive IX). This file is sealed law: any byte change re-pins PINNED_FRAME_QUESTIONS_SHA256 and re-sits teeth 13-15 before the frame courts may sit again. Forced binaries only — the confidence scalar is a logged diagnostic and decides nothing.

questions.closure: A painting was briefed to contain EXACTLY these named figures and no other figures: {SUBJECTS}. {ALLOWANCE} Count the distinct human figures in this painting that read as identifiable individuals — a figure with a readable face, or one rendered prominently enough to read as a specific person rather than scenery. Silhouettes glimpsed through windows or doorways, figures lost in deep background shadow, and indistinct souls that read as scenery belong to the crowd, never to this count. Does that count exactly equal the number of named figures? Answer ONLY with JSON: {"figures_match": true or false, "counted": <integer, your count of identifiable figures>, "unaccounted": "<describe any extra figure the brief does not name, or any named figure missing from the frame; empty string if none>", "confidence": <0..1>}
questions.allowance_none: NO background crowd was granted: every human figure of any prominence, foreground or background, must be one of the named figures.
questions.allowance_background: A background crowd WAS granted: distant, indistinct, unidentifiable background figures are lawful and are NOT counted — count only the identifiable figures.
questions.principal_box: Box the single most prominent human figure in this painting — the figure foremost in the composition by size, position, and focus. Answer ONLY with JSON: {"found": true or false, "x": <0..1>, "y": <0..1>, "w": <0..1>, "h": <0..1>} — normalized fractions of image width and height, the box tight around that one figure and including its head. If no human figure is present, answer found: false.
questions.principal_identity: This image is a crop showing the most prominent figure of a larger painting. The painting's brief named its principal figure as: {CLAUSE}. Does the cropped figure match that identity — plausibly the same person, consistent with every stated trait of the clause? Answer ONLY with JSON: {"figure_matches": true or false, "mismatch": "<name the stated trait the figure fails, or empty string>", "confidence": <0..1>}
questions.fixture_pair: Two paintings depict the same place, painted independently of one another. The place’s sealed canon includes this standing fixture: {VISUAL}. Is that fixture visibly present in BOTH paintings — in each painting an object of the canon’s kind, material, scale, and distinctive form, recognizably the sealed fixture? Independent paintings lawfully vary their staging: exact mounting or attachment, viewing angle, position within the ground, surface finish as lit, and incidental dressing are NOT inconsistencies. Refuse only when the fixture is missing from either painting, is a different kind of object, or is unrecognizable as the canon fixture. Answer ONLY with JSON: {"fixture_present_in_both": true or false, "inconsistency": "<name what is missing or unrecognizable, or empty string>", "confidence": <0..1>}
questions.sheet_integrity: This image is expected to be a working MODEL SHEET: ONE image arranged as a 2x2 grid of four cells — four studio views of the same subject (such as a face front-on, a profile, a full figure, and a close detail) on plain neutral backdrops. Painters vary gutters and borders; a readable four-cell 2x2 arrangement is what counts, not printed frame lines. The sheet must also be FREE OF LETTERING: no labels, letters, numerals, watermarks, captions, or writing of any kind anywhere on the image. Answer ONLY with JSON: {"grid_four_cells": true or false, "free_of_lettering": true or false, "flaw": "<name the lettering you see or the way the grid fails, or empty string>", "confidence": <0..1>}
questions.sheet_identity: This image is a MODEL SHEET of four cells depicting one subject. The sheet was minted for exactly this subject: {CLAUSE}. Two questions, each true or false: subject_matches — the depicted subject is consistent with every stated trait of that identity (its kind, its mark or defining detail, its attire or covering, its palette); cells_agree — all four cells depict the SAME subject (same face or form, same costume or covering) rather than different subjects. Neutral poses and angle changes between cells are the sheet's purpose, never a mismatch. Judge stated traits by their clear depiction at painting scale: a mark or detail is PRESENT when its form is plainly shown; never fail subject_matches over the exact count of repeated small elements (shards, links, studs, buttons) that cell resolution cannot settle, so long as the element itself is depicted. Answer ONLY with JSON: {"subject_matches": true or false, "cells_agree": true or false, "mismatch": "<name the stated trait that fails or the cell that disagrees, or empty string>", "confidence": <0..1>}
questions.attire_pair: Two paintings depict the SAME character. The character's sealed attire canon is: {ATTIRE}. Is that attire visibly worn in BOTH paintings — the same garment class and palette in each, allowing lawful variation in pose, lighting, framing, and partial occlusion? A close bust may crop below the shoulders: judge only what is visible, and refuse ONLY on a visible CONTRADICTION — a clearly different garment class, a clearly different palette, or dress that plainly is not the canon attire. Answer ONLY with JSON: {"attire_consistent": true or false, "contradiction": "<name the visible contradiction, or empty string>", "confidence": <0..1>}
